triples.object: United States v. Apple may refer to:

triples.subject: Net neutrality
Digital Divide
triples.object: Digital divide is unequal access to and use of digital technology , encompassing four interrelated dimensions: motivational, material, skills, and usage access. [ 1 ]

triples.subject: Online censorship
Deepfake
Digital surveillance
Content moderation
Data breach
Intellectual property laws
Online activism
triples.object: Internet activism [ a ] involves the use of electronic-communication technologies such as social media , e-mail , and podcasts for various forms of activism to enable faster and more effective communication by citizen movements , the delivery of particular information to large and specific audiences

triples.subject: Cyberbullying
triples.object: Cyberbullying ( cyberharassment or online bullying / harassment ) is a form of bullying or harassment using electronic means. Since the 2000s, it has become increasingly common, especially among teenagers and adolescents , due to young people's increased use of social media. [ 1 ] Related issues inc

triples.subject: Cyber warfare
